case.title: TRANS-LUX DIST. CORP. v. BOARD OF REGENTS UNIV. N. Y.

opinion.h: TRANS-LUX DIST. CORP. v. BOARD OF REGENTS UNIV. N. Y., 380 U.S. 259 (1965)
TRANS-LUX DISTRIBUTING CORP. v. BOARD OF REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 314. Decided March 15, 1965.
opinion.p: The judgment is reversed. Freedman v. Maryland, ante, p. 51.
380 U.S. 259 SANTOS v. TEXAS. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE COURT OF CRIMINAL APPEALS OF TEXAS. No. 340, Misc. Decided March 15, 1965.
Certiorari granted, judgment vacated and case remanded.
Waggoner Carr, Attorney General of Texas, and Howard M. Fender, Gilbert J. Pena and Allo B. Crow, Jr., Assistant Attorneys General, for respondent.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the Court of Criminal Appeals of Texas for further consideration in light of the opinion of this Court in Aguilar v. Texas, 378 U.S. 108.
